utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality outputs that passed all verifier checks and addressed most evaluation criteria. Both correctly identified parasitic drain as the primary hypothesis, sequenced diagnostics correctly, described a methodologically sound parasitic draw isolation procedure, addressed the post-lock hum diagnostically, and differentiated the customer and technician documents. Both failed J3-6 (cost-effectiveness guidance for the customer). The wi_skills response is marginally stronger in the following ways: (1) the alternator-battery failure chain is more explicitly articulated — the internal note specifically warns against pre-ordering the battery and discusses secondary battery damage risk more concretely; (2) the internal tech note references Ford-specific module terminology (BCM/SJB), which aligns with the expected 'module-sourced draws on 2013-era Ford trucks' flag; (3) the customer response is longer and more thorough in explaining why 'battery tested good' does not rule out the problem. However, the wo_skills output is surprisingly competitive — the no-skill agent produced a very well-structured and technically sound response that rivals the skill-equipped agent in most categories. Overall: wi_skills is slightly more effective due to greater Ford-specific technical depth and more explicit failure-chain reasoning, but the difference is modest.

utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality diagnostic reports that passed all 6 evaluation criteria. (5) The wo_skills report, while meeting all criteria, includes a specific TSB reference (Honda TSB 19-011) that appears to be fabricated/hallucinated, as no network search was performed to verify it — this is a potential accuracy risk. Overall, wi_skills produces a more polished, customer-ready document that better reflects expert-level automotive diagnostic reasoning.
